NWPD Search for Missing Person Christopher
content:
New Westminster –
The New Westminster Police Department is seeking public assistance in locating a missing person, Christopher.
Christopher is described as a 31 year-old Indigenous male, 5’10” tall, medium build, short brown hair and brown eyes. Christopher also wears eye glasses.
“We simply want to confirm Christopher’s well-being,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “His loved ones are worried that they haven’t heard from him.”
Christopher may be taking public transit and has also been known to spend time in Vancouver. If you have any information about Christopher’s location, please call the New Westminster Police Department at 604-525-5411. If you see Christopher we ask that you call 9-1-1.
-30-
MEDIA CONTACT PERSON: Sergeant Andrew Leaver
MEDIA EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: Sexual Exploitation Investigation Leads Police to Multiple Weapons Seizure
content:
New Westminster –
On March 21
st
, 2026 a concerned citizen reported seeing what they believed to be an adult male acting in an inappropriate manner with a female youth in a public place. The male and female left the area, however the concerned citizen obtained the license plate of their vehicle and called police.
New Westminster Police Department officers responded and located the male, although he was not in the presence of the female youth. Officers established that this male has conditions not to be in the presence of anyone under the age of 16, and although the female he had been seen with was a youth, he was not in breach of his conditions.
The Street Crime Unit assumed conduct of the investigation, and after a detailed investigation, the male was arrested for Sexual Exploitation. At the time of the arrest, the male was wearing a cross-body bag that was found to contain a bear scare fire pistol. Further investigation led officers to believe the male may be in possession of other weapons. The Street Crime Unit obtained a search warrant for the male’s home, and subsequently located a SKS rifle, a pistol-gripped compact shot gun, a 22 caliber semi-automatic rifle, ammunition, replica firearms, holsters, 3D printed firearm parts, and a number of other prohibited devices.
“The presence of weapons in relation to a sexual exploitation investigation is extremely concerning” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “We are thankful to the concerned citizen who called us and allowed us to uncover not only a multitude of weapons, but to also remove a vulnerable female from a dangerous situation”.
On May 26th, 2026 Jorge Reyes Alfaro of New Westminster was charged with:
Sexual exploitation contrary to section 153(1)(a) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Unauthorized possession of a prohibited weapon or restricted weapon contrary to section 91(2) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Possession of a weapon for dangerous purpose contrary to section 88(1) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Carrying a concealed weapon contrary to section 90(1) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Weapons Trafficking contrary to section 99(1)(a) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm contrary to section 91(1) of the Criminal Code of Canada;
Possession of computer data contrary to section 102.1(1) of the Criminal Code of Canada; and,
Contravention of storage regulations contrary to section 86(2) of the Criminal Code of Canada
Reyes Alfaro remains in custody as police continue to process the evidence. “This is a great reminder to the public to call us if something doesn’t seem or feel right”, stated Sergeant Leaver.
The New Westminster Police Department would like to remind the public to call police if they see suspicious activity. The New Westminster Police Department can be reached on the non-emergency line at 604-525-5411, or in the event of an emergency, please call 9-1-1.
-30-
CONTACT PERSON: Sergeant Andrew Leaver
EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: Man with replica handgun sparks heavy police response near New Westminster SkyTrain Station
content:
New Westminster – On May 26th, 2026 at approximately 8:15pm a passerby noticed a male who they believed to be in possession of a handgun in the parking garage at 800 Carnarvon Street, New Westminster. The New Westminster Police Department responded and contained the area.
The male was safely taken into custody and found to be in possession of a holstered replica firearm and magazines. The male was transported to hospital to be treated for existing injuries and was released on an undertaking with numerous firearm-related conditions.
The incident caused a temporary disruption to the area, including the transit system. Police would like to thank the public for their patience while this matter was being resolved. “These types of incidents are extremely dynamic, and measures were put into place to make sure that the public were kept safe as the event unfolded” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. This incident required numerous police resources, and the New Westminster Police Department would like to thank the RCMP Integrated Police Dog Services, the Metro Vancouver Transit Police, and the Burnaby RCMP for their assistance.
When police respond to any call involving a firearm, it is treated as legitimate until an investigation can determine otherwise. “These replicas look extremely realistic, and being in possession of them can have serious consequences” stated Sergeant Leaver. For the safety of our communities, the New Westminster Police Department strongly discourages the purchase and use of these items.
-30-
CONTACT PERSON: Andrew Leaver
EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: Multiple vehicles taken out of service during Commercial Vehicle Enforcement event
content:
New Westminster –
On May 13
th
, 2026, the New Westminster Police Department Traffic Unit, along with other community safety partners, conducted a Commercial Vehicle safety inspection event along the 500 block of Royal Avenue in New Westminster.
Working alongside the Port Moody Police Department, Delta Police Department, Metro Vancouver Transit Police, the RCMP Integrated Road Safety Unit, and Langley Township Bylaw Commercial Vehicle Enforcement, officers use their specialized expertise to inspect 23 commercial vehicles.
Of the vehicles that were inspected, 8 were taken out of service due to various mechanical or safety concerns, 4 vehicles were found to have insecure loads, and 1 vehicle was determined to be overweight. In total, 24 Provincial Violation tickets were issued for infractions noted during the inspections.
“We all have a part to play in keeping our roads safe,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “We’re grateful to work alongside our community safety partners, who bring valuable expertise to these checks. Their knowledge and training help ensure we’re identifying serious risks before they lead to a collision.”
Police would like to remind commercial vehicle operators to ensure that their vehicles are in compliant operating conditions before starting their trips.
The New Westminster Police Department thanks all partner agencies involved for helping make this enforcement initiative a success.
-30-
CONTACT PERSON: Sergeant Andrew Leaver
EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: Officers arrest armed man after woman stabbed
content:
New Westminster –
On May 6 at approximately 11:00pm the New Westminster Police Department received a 9-1-1 call from someone describing a woman being slashed with a knife. The caller shared that two men were fleeing the scene and they provided descriptions of the suspects. Officers rushed to the New Westminster SkyTrain station to provide aid to the woman who was found with a laceration.
Within minutes, officers located two men matching the suspect descriptions on Columbia Street. When officers called out to the men, they split up and ran in different directions. After two foot pursuits the suspects quickly found themselves in police custody. One of the suspects was found to be in possession of a loaded handgun, drugs, and several thousands of dollars of counterfeit currency.
“We’d like to thank the caller who made the decision to call 9-1-1,” stated Media Relations Officer Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “Anyone can make the choice to be an active bystander and help victims of crime by relaying important information about what you’re seeing to police.”
The victim was transported to hospital by BC Emergency Health Services Paramedics. At this stage in the investigation, it is unclear what relationship, if any, exists between the victim and the suspects.
-30-
MEDIA CONTACT PERSON: Hailey Finnigan
MEDIA EMAIL:
[email protected]
FILE: 26-7454

title: Queensborough Bridge southbound lanes closed due to vehicle fire
content:
New Westminster –
On May 8 at approximately 2:00pm New Westminster Police Department officers were called to assist New Westminster Fire and Rescue Services with traffic control after a vehicle caught fire on the Queenborough Bridge.
Crews are working to clear fuel and debris on the bridge, however it is anticipated the southbound lanes of the Queensborough Bridge will remain closed for several hours.
Commuters are asked to find an alternate route and first responders thank everyone for their patience. Pedestrian and cyclist access to the bridge has been maintained.
-30-
CONTACT PERSON: Hailey Finnigan
MEDIA EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: NWPD search for missing person Freweini
content:
Update – Freweini has been located and is safe.
New Westminster –
The New Westminster Police Department is seeking public assistance in locating a missing person, Freweini.
Freweini is described as a 42 year old woman, standing 5’7” tall, with black hair, a slim build, and brown eyes.
“We simply want to confirm Freweini’s well-being,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “We want to ensure she is safe.”
Freweini may be taking public transit. If you have any information about Freweini’s location, please call the New Westminster Police Department at 604-525-5411. If you see Freweini we ask that you call 9-1-1 without delay.

title: Multiple reports made after man seen with handgun-style lighter
content:
New Westminster –
On April 21
st
at approximately 1:00 pm a member of the public attended the front counter of the New Westminster Police Department to share they saw someone who they believed to be in possession of a handgun on Clarkson Street. Moments later, a call was made to 9-1-1 describing a similar suspect with a black handgun.
Officers quickly located a man on Clarkson Street matching the suspect description. An NWPD drone was deployed to get a closer look at the suspect. The footage from the NWPD drone helped officers in determining the handgun was a butane lighter shaped like a handgun. The use of the NWPD drone avoided what would have been a much larger and higher-risk police response.
“These handgun-shaped lighters continue to be a source of calls to 9-1-1,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “When someone brandishes something that looks like a handgun in a public setting they’re putting themselves and everyone around them at risk.”
Officers had a conversation with the man about the distress his butane lighter caused people in the area. The man handed the lighter to police for disposal. Easily mistaken for real firearms, handgun-shaped lighters can escalate police interactions and prompt 9-1-1 calls that are prioritized, resulting in elevated police response.
-30-
MEDIA CONTACT PERSON: Hailey Finnigan
MEDIA EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: Help us find high risk missing person Colleen
content:
Update:
Missing person Colleen has been located and is safe.
New Westminster –
The New Westminster Police Department is seeking public assistance in the search for missing person Colleen. Colleen has dementia and may appear disoriented.
Colleen was last seen on April 21 near near 9th Street and 3rd Avenue in New Westminster. Colleen is described as a 74 year-old female, with shoulder length blonde grey hair in a claw clip, wearing a blue sweater, black pants, and blue running shoes.
“We are asking the public to help us locate Colleen,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “Colleen’s family is very concerned about her well-being.”
If you see Colleen investigators ask that you call 9-1-1 immediately and stay with her until police arrive.
-30-
MEDIA CONTACT PERSON: Sergeant Andrew Leaver
MEDIA EMAIL:
[email protected]

title: NWPD search for missing person Gordon “Dale” Raymond who was last seen in 1987
content:
New Westminster –
The New Westminster Police Department is seeking public assistance in determining the whereabouts of Gordon Raymond who was last seen in 1987. His family and friends have not seen or heard from him since. Mr. Raymond was commonly known as “Dale,” and sometimes “Gord.”
Mr. Raymond was 39 years old at the time of his disappearance and would be 79 years old today. In the 1980s he lived throughout the Metro Vancouver region, including Surrey, Vancouver, and New Westminster. He has previously lived in Edmonton, Alberta and Thailand. He is described as a Caucasian male, standing 6’2” tall, with top and bottom false teeth, and surgery scars from a broken hip and an appendectomy. He was known to work as an aircraft mechanic and drove a cream 1976 Buick Century, similar to the one pictured below. In 1986 he painted the rear windows of his car black, and may have slept in his vehicle. Mr. Raymond had a son, Gordon Jr., who police are hoping to speak to.
“We are committed to supporting this investigation and finding Mr. Raymond,” stated Sergeant Andrew Leaver. “His family is hoping for answers. Please, be a force multiplier and let’s work together to share this appeal for information.”
Mr. Raymond’s sister Colleen had the following to share about her brother:
I remember when my big brother Gord, who eventually preferred to go by Dale, surprised me by showing up at my school choir’s performance in a distant city, where he lived at the time. He walked up to the stage and waved, before we started singing, so I knew he was there for me when our parents couldn’t be. He hasn’t shown up for the past 40 years, but our family has never stopped hoping he will. If anyone has any information about Dale, no matter how small, please call the detectives. It could make all the difference.
This is the performance Colleen remembers when Dale surprised her.
https://www.nwpolice.org/media/School-choir-performance-1969-1970.m4a
The Specialized Investigations Unit works to progress historical missing persons investigations. Investigators have recently been able to dedicate focused time to advancing this file. If you have any information about Mr. Raymond, please call the New Westminster Police Department at 604-525-5411.
A car similar to the one owned by missing person Gordon Dale Raymond.
-30-
